content.full_text:
Re
Fern PoLrrrcAL PnacrrcES CouvrrssroN
428 J Street. Suite 620. Sacramento, CA 95814-2329
(916) 322-5660. Fax (916) 322-0886
May 6,2003
David Bauer
400 Capitol Mall, Suite 1560
Sacramento, CA 95814
Your Request for Informal Advice
Our FiIe No. I-02-353
Dear Mr. Bauer:
QUESTION
What are the attribution requirements for transfers from a state legislative
candidate's committee created by Assemblymember Leslie to his future committees for
other offices?
CONCLUSION
As discussed below, transfers of funds held on January l,2OOl, may be made
without attribution. Transfers of funds held by the candidate after that date must be
attributed according to section 85306, subdivision (a).
F'ACTS
Tim Leslie was re-elected to the Assembly in2002 and his committee for that
campaign, Tim Leslie for Assembly, ended the campaign with substantial funds and no
debt. In our phone conversation, you indicated that some of those funds were on hand on
I Government Code sections 81000 - gl}14. Commission regulations appear at Title 2, sections
18109-18997, of the California Code of Regulations.
2 Pursuant to enclosed Regulation 18329(c)(3) (enclosed), informal assistance does not confer
immunity.
This letter is in response to your request for advice on behalf of the Tim Leslie for
Assembly committee regarding the campaign provisions of the Political Reform Act (the
"Act").' Since your inquiry is general in nature, we are treating your request as one for
informal assistance.z (Reg. 1 8329OX8XC).)
:::


File No. l-02-353
Page No. 2
January 1,2001. Some of those funds have been transferred without attribution to Tim
Leslie forAssembly 2004, and more will be. In addition to the transferred funds, there
will be ongoing fundraising efforts on behalf of the2004 committee. It is possible that
after the 2004 election, there will be funds remaining in the 2004 committee in excess of
the funds raised by that committee. Essentially, the transfers from the 2002 committee
could exceed the costs of operatingthe2004 committee.
Assernblyman Leslie may form a committeein2}}4 to run for a different office
in2006 and transfer funds from the 2004 c,ommittee to that2O06 committee. It is possible
that the funds transferred from2004 to 2006 will exceed the total of funds raised by the
2004committee. Forinstance, the20O2 committeemaytransfer$100,000 tothe2004
committee and the 2004 committee may raise an additional $100,000. Ttre2004
committee may spend only $50,000 so that the balance to be transferred to the 2006
committee would be $150,000.
ANALYSIS
Section 85306 of the Act governs transfers between a candidate's own
committees. That statute states:
"$ 85306. Transfers Between a Candidaters Own Committeesl
Use of Funds Raised Prior to Effective Date.
"(a) A candidate may transfer campaign funds from one controlled
committee to a controlled committee for elective state office of the
same candidate. Contributions transferred shall be attributed to
specific contributors using a 'last in, first out' or 'first in, first out'
accounting method, and these attributed contributions when
aggregated with all other contributions from the same contributor
may not exceed the limits set forth in Section 85301 or 85302.
"(b) Notwithstanding subdivision (a), a candidate for elective state
office, other than a candidate for statewide elective office, who
possesses campaign funds on January 1,2001,may use those funds
to seek elective office without attributing the funds to specific
contributors.
"(c) Notwithstanding subdivision (a), a candidate for statewide
elective office who possesses campaign funds on November 6,2002,
may use those funds to seek elective office without attributing the
funds to specific contributors."
Because the transfer of funds from the 2002 committee to the20O4 and2006
committees may be comprised of funds possessed on January l, 2OOl, subdivision (b) of
section 85306 states those funds may be transferred without attribution. The Commission
recently interpreted that subdivision to mean that a candidate may transfer such funds an
unlimited number of times in the future to the candidate's own prospective committee


File No. I-02-353
Page No. 3
until the funds are expended. The amount of unattributed funds expended may not be
thereafter replenished by subsequent contributions to the committee. To the extent the
receiving committee's balance falls below the amount transferred to it, then, the amount
thatmay be transferred in the future is reduced to that amount. (See Emerg.Reg.
18530.2, subd. (a), copy enclosed.)3 For instance, if the 2002 committee transfer of
$100,000 above is comprised completely of funds possessed by the committee on January
1,2001, then Assemblyman Leslie may transfer those funds to the 2004 committee
without attribution. ($ 85306, subd. (b).) If the 20A4 cornmittee then raises additional
funds and makes subsequent expenditures, the 2004 committee may transfer the 2002
funds it received ($100,000) without attribution to the 2006 committee so long as the
2004 committee balance never dipped below $100,000. To the extent the committee's
balance falls below $100,000, that money may not be "replenished" and the amount that
may be transferred by the 2004 committee without attribution is thereby reduced.
For funds that came into possession of the 2002 committee after January l,20Ol,
and for all other funds raised by the 2004 and 2006 committees, subdivision (a) of section
85306 governs. Accordingly, those funds must be attributed to specific conkibutors
using the "first in, first out" or "last in, first out" method of accounting. (See Reg.
18536.) The exception to this rule is contained in section 85317, which provides that a
candidate may carry over contributions raised in connection with one election for elective
state office to pay campaign expenditures incurred in connection with a subsequent
election for the same elective state office. ($ 85317.) Regulation 18537 .1, interpreting
this statute, is included for your information.
If you have any other questions regarding this rnatter,please contact me at (916)
322-s660.
Sincerely,
Luisa
General
By: C. Scott
Counsel, Legal Division
cSrjg

Enclosures
meeting.
3 It is anticipated that the Commission will adoptpermanently this regulation at its May g,2003

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: What are the attribution requirements for transfers from a state legislative
candidate's committee created by Assemblymember Leslie to his future committees for
other offices?

CONCLUSION: As discussed below, transfers of funds held on January l,2OOl, may be made
without attribution. Transfers of funds held by the candidate after that date must be
attributed according to section 85306, subdivision (a).
